scope: Diagnose why the AI assistant caused Clippy to lock up (100% CPU, ~2GB RAM, force-quit). Diagnosis only; no fix applied.

findings.title: Streaming AI bubble uses .textSelection(.enabled) on live-mutating Text, causing a non-converging SwiftUI AttributeGraph update loop on the main thread
findings.culprit: Sources/Clippy/AI/AIAssistantPanelView.swift:498 (live Text) and :509 (wrapping Group); spinner at :394; text appended per token at :116
findings.mechanism: Each streamed token mutates @Published messages[i].text (:116) -> SwiftUI rebuilds the selectable Text -> SelectionOverlay.updateNSView recomputes AppKit text geometry (setFont / NSAttributedString.replacingLineBreakModes) -> AppKit layout invalidation re-enters the AttributeGraph transaction (NSHostingView.beginTransaction -> GraphHost.flushTransactions -> AG::Subgraph::update -> PlatformViewRepresentableAdaptor.updateViewProvider) -> never reaches a fixed point while tokens arrive -> main thread pinned ~100%, run loop never completes, footprint grows unbounded.
evidence: docs/evidence/spindump-2026-06-15-heaviest-stack.txt (Footprint 147->2105 MB, Unresponsive, 1 thread, 88% CPU, leaf SelectionOverlay.updateNSView)
docs/evidence/spindump-2026-06-16-heaviest-stack.txt (Footprint 100->1611 MB, Unresponsive, 1 thread, 96% CPU, leaf SelectionOverlay.updateNSView -> setFont)
docs/evidence/unified-log-ollama-stream-2026-06-17.txt (freeze session: Ollama :11434 turns C45 55.96s + C46 64.68s/48.9KB cancelled 02:23:09 at force-quit; Clippy log volume collapses after 02:22)

verifier.verdict: source + spindump stacks VERIFIED; alternatives (markdown re-parse, per-token autoscroll, network/agent loop) independently RULED OUT
alternatives_ruled_out: Markdown re-parse per token: Markdown(...) only renders when !isLive (code comment + branch at :491-500)
ScrollViewReader autoscroll per token: onChange triggers on messages.count and state only (:373,:378), not per token; tokens mutate text in place
Network/AIAgent infinite loop: both spindumps show Num threads:1 pinned inside SwiftUI layout, not networking/async
caveats: Verifier could not read raw .diag from its context (group _analyticsusers); primary session confirmed raw files readable and extracts faithful.
Original log evidence captured the wrong Ollama connection (C19 @00:17). CORRECTED 2026-06-17: file now shows freeze-session C45/C46 @02:21-02:23.

fix.applied_where: Sources/Clippy/AI/AIAssistantPanelView.swift (working tree, NOT committed): removed .textSelection from the isLive Text branch; replaced the Group-level .textSelection(.enabled) with .textSelectionEnabled(!isLive); added a private View.textSelectionEnabled(_:) helper that returns self (no SelectionOverlay) when disabled.
fix.runtime_result: Fixed binary (ad-hoc signed /tmp/ClippyTest.app) run for ~50 min of real use. A streaming Ollama turn (proven via log: conn C26, 60.4s) ran inside a monitored window: PEAK 9.3% CPU, RSS flat 314 MB, 0 samples over 50% CPU, no new cpu_resource.diag. Contrast: old v1.5 pinned 88-96% CPU and grew to 1.6-2.1 GB on a comparable 64s stream.
fix.caveat: Largest stream reproduced under the fix this session was 1.6 KB in / 60s. Tonight's freeze trigger was 48.9 KB in / 64s. A same-magnitude (~48 KB) heavy stream was not reproduced, so the runtime proof is strong-but-not-airtight for the heaviest case. The 60s turn did exercise the per-token render path the fix removes, and stayed flat.
fix.not_done: No same-magnitude (~48 KB) stream captured under the fix.
fix.to_close_airtight: Stream a >=40 KB response (long essay to a fast local model) while running docs/evidence runtime watcher; expect CPU to stay low and RSS flat, no cpu_resource.diag.
